hypothesis: A hybrid batching schedule that uses dynamic batching for the first 100,000 training steps and switches to static-64 thereafter will reduce end-to-end wall-clock training time by at least 12% relative to static-64 alone, without a statistically significant change in final test RMSE (p > 0.10), for the PaiNN model on both AFLOW and QM9 at batch size 64 on a single A100 GPU with JAX.
